Title: Aflatoxin-free transgenic maize using host-induced gene silencing

Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: GenBank: L42766; qRT-PCR target accession AY510451.1
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: B73 × A188 (Hi II hybrid background); null B73 hybrid controls used in assays
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: A. flavus isolate AF13 used for infection; RNAi cassette designed from A. parasiticus aflC sequence
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: RNAi cassette expressed in developing kernels at 10–12 DAP; target suppression evaluated in infected kernels 30 days after infection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: aflC transcript detected during fungal infection of maize kernels; strongly reduced in RNAi kernels versus null controls
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Polyketide synthase; aflatoxin biosynthesis protein PKSA
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Toxic fungal secondary metabolite causing major food safety and health problems
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Host-induced silencing of aflC eliminated detectable aflatoxin accumulation in infected transgenic maize kernels
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Kernel-specific RNAi transgene expression; Aspergillus infection assay; qRT-PCR of aflC transcript; TLC aflatoxin quantification
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: A. flavus AF13 infection of developing cobs at 8–10 DAP under greenhouse conditions
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No substantial morphological changes or consistent transcriptome alterations detected in transgenic kernels
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value HIGS target for engineering aflatoxin-free maize and potentially other crops
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A kernel-specific RNAi cassette targeting fungal aflC was expressed in transgenic maize kernels. 2. aflC transcript abundance was strongly reduced in infected RNAi kernels. 3. Aflatoxin was undetectable in RNAi kernels despite comparable fungal presence, supporting selective suppression of toxin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is the direct experimental target of the study, but its function is exploited through HIGS rather than engineering the fungal genome directly.
